speaker: WARWICK
text: Where is the post that came from valiant Oxford?—

speaker: WARWICK
text: How far hence is thy lord, mine honest fellow?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MESSENGER
text: By this at Dunsmore, marching hitherward.

speaker: WARWICK
text: How far off is our brother Montague?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Where is the post that came from Montague?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: By this at Daintry, with a puissant troop.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Say, Somerville, what says my loving son?

speaker: WARWICK
text: And, by thy guess, how nigh is Clarence now?

speaker: SOMERVILLE
text: At Southam I did leave him with his forces

speaker: SOMERVILLE
text: And do expect him here some two hours hence.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Then Clarence is at hand; I hear his drum.

speaker: SOMERVILLE
text: It is not his, my lord; here Southam lies.

speaker: SOMERVILLE
text: The drum your Honor hears marcheth from Warwick.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Who should that be? Belike unlooked-for friends.

speaker: SOMERVILLE
text: They are at hand, and you shall quickly know.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Go, Trumpet, to the walls, and sound a parle.

speaker: RICHARD
text: See how the surly Warwick mans the wall.

speaker: WARWICK
text: O unbid spite, is sportful Edward come?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Where slept our scouts, or how are they seduced,

speaker: WARWICK
text: That we could hear no news of his repair?

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Now, Warwick, wilt thou ope the city gates,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Speak gentle words, and humbly bend thy knee?

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Call Edward king, and at his hands beg mercy,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And he shall pardon thee these outrages.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Nay, rather wilt thou draw thy forces hence,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Confess who set thee up and plucked thee down,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Call Warwick patron, and be penitent,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And thou shalt still remain the Duke of York.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I thought at least he would have said “the King.”

speaker: RICHARD
text: Or did he make the jest against his will?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Is not a dukedom, sir, a goodly gift?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ay, by my faith, for a poor earl to give.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I’ll do thee service for so good a gift.

speaker: WARWICK
text: ’Twas I that gave the kingdom to thy brother.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Why, then, ’tis mine, if but by Warwick’s gift.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Thou art no Atlas for so great a weight;

speaker: WARWICK
text: And, weakling, Warwick takes his gift again,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And Henry is my king, Warwick his subject.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: But Warwick’s king is Edward’s prisoner.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And, gallant Warwick, do but answer this:

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: What is the body when the head is off?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Alas, that Warwick had no more forecast,

speaker: RICHARD
text: But whiles he thought to steal the single ten,

speaker: RICHARD
text: The King was slyly fingered from the deck.

speaker: RICHARD
text: You left poor Henry at the Bishop’s palace,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And ten to one you’ll meet him in the Tower.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: ’Tis even so; yet you are Warwick still.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Come, Warwick, take the time; kneel down, kneel down.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Nay, when? Strike now, or else the iron cools.

speaker: WARWICK
text: I had rather chop this hand off at a blow

speaker: WARWICK
text: And with the other fling it at thy face

speaker: WARWICK
text: Than bear so low a sail to strike to thee.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Sail how thou canst, have wind and tide thy friend,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: This hand, fast wound about thy coal-black hair,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Shall, whiles thy head is warm and new cut off,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Write in the dust this sentence with thy blood:

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: “Wind-changing Warwick now can change no more.”

speaker: WARWICK
text: O, cheerful colors, see where Oxford comes!

speaker: OXFORD
text: Oxford, Oxford for Lancaster!

speaker: RICHARD
text: The gates are open; let us enter too.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: So other foes may set upon our backs.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Stand we in good array, for they no doubt

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Will issue out again and bid us battle.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: If not, the city being but of small defense,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: We’ll quickly rouse the traitors in the same.

speaker: WARWICK
text: O welcome, Oxford, for we want thy help.

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: Montague, Montague for Lancaster!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thou and thy brother both shall buy this treason

speaker: RICHARD
text: Even with the dearest blood your bodies bear!

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: The harder matched, the greater victory.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: My mind presageth happy gain and conquest.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Somerset, Somerset for Lancaster!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Two of thy name, both dukes of Somerset,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Have sold their lives unto the house of York,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And thou shalt be the third, if this sword hold.

speaker: WARWICK
text: And lo, where George of Clarence sweeps along,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Of force enough to bid his brother battle,

speaker: WARWICK
text: With whom an upright zeal to right prevails

speaker: WARWICK
text: More than the nature of a brother’s love.—

speaker: WARWICK
text: Come, Clarence, come; thou wilt, if Warwick call.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Father of Warwick, know you what this means?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Look, here I throw my infamy at thee.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: I will not ruinate my father’s house,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Who gave his blood to lime the stones together

speaker: CLARENCE
text: And set up Lancaster. Why, trowest thou, Warwick,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: That Clarence is so harsh, so blunt, unnatural,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: To bend the fatal instruments of war

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Against his brother and his lawful king?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Perhaps thou wilt object my holy oath.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: To keep that oath were more impiety

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Than Jephthah when he sacrificed his daughter.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: I am so sorry for my trespass made

speaker: CLARENCE
text: That, to deserve well at my brother’s hands,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: I here proclaim myself thy mortal foe,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: With resolution, wheresoe’er I meet thee—

speaker: CLARENCE
text: As I will meet thee if thou stir abroad—

speaker: CLARENCE
text: To plague thee for thy foul misleading me.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: And so, proud-hearted Warwick, I defy thee

speaker: CLARENCE
text: And to my brother turn my blushing cheeks.—

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Pardon me, Edward, I will make amends.—

speaker: CLARENCE
text: And, Richard, do not frown upon my faults,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: For I will henceforth be no more unconstant.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Now, welcome more, and ten times more beloved,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Than if thou never hadst deserved our hate.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Welcome, good Clarence; this is brother-like.

speaker: WARWICK
text: O, passing traitor, perjured and unjust.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: What, Warwick, wilt thou leave the town and fight?

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Or shall we beat the stones about thine ears?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Alas, I am not cooped here for defense.

speaker: WARWICK
text: I will away towards Barnet presently

speaker: WARWICK
text: And bid thee battle, Edward, if thou dar’st.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Yes, Warwick, Edward dares, and leads the way.—

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Lords, to the field! Saint George and victory!
